返回本批次

秘密之盒：谨慎改装公寓对讲机以与 Apple Home 配合使用

Box of Secrets: Discreetly modding an apartment intercom to work with Apple Home

www.jackhogan.me 作者 jackhogan11 分数 86 评论 16 2026/03/23 20:42
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 我的朋友弗兰克（化名）在他的公寓接待了很多客人，他的公寓大楼的对讲机负责引导他们进来。您可能以前见过它们，它们看起来像这样：弗兰克公寓的 Doorking 1834-080 对讲机。直到最近，客人还可以在系统中找到弗兰克的电话号码并拨打电话。如果弗兰克认出了线路上的人，他会在拨号盘上按一个数字，控制器会将其解释为解锁大门的信号。然后，管理层就变得懒惰了。弗兰克居住的大楼未能更新对讲机的蜂窝服务，因此无法再拨打语音系统。即使在要求房东修理几个月后，也没有任何进展。救援到来 我和我的另一位朋友黑泽尔在停电期间拜访了弗兰克，他让我们看看能做些什么。这是我们所看到的：我们所看到的图表。我们仔细检查了顶部的盒子，给出了一个令人鼓舞的结果：它被解锁了！音箱的总体布局如下： 所示为音箱的接线图。不可能忽视顶部角落里巨大的 Wi-Fi/蜂窝路由器，其管理员密码就印在上面（未显示）。当然，我必须调查一下。尝试 1：Wi-Fi 路由器 我很快找到了网络并输入了显示的登录凭据。当然，它们并没有改变默认值。我拥有路由器的完全管理员访问权限，这太棒了，直到我意识到我无法使用其基本的锁定界面做太多事情。这几乎结束了我的探索，但后来我意识到：SSH 怎么样？ AT&T 是为 Doorking 制造路由器的公司，它比一袋石头更聪明，因为 SSH 在他们的路由器上受到保护。对他们来说可悲的是，他们输给了石头袋，提供了一种从 Web 界面下载整个系统配置的方法，其中包含将 root 密码重置为您想要的任何内容的方法： # 该文件是从 NetComm Bovine 平台设备导出的配置。 # 私有字段已加密，但任何配置条目都可以手动替换为 # 纯文本变量或 URI 编码文本。 admin.firewall.enable;1 admin.local.enable_http;1 admin.local.enable_https;1 admin.local.ssh_enable;1 admin.local.telnetenable;1 admin.open.port;管理员密码； admin.user.admin;$aM9VdmCoc5vuekVU70/Gl8iJTOujxMQo admin.user.root;$DDDgp0GJy6nB29UX7pDlrUUKDkWYqp84
  2. 哇。我现在明白为什么路由器漏洞如此常见了。
  3. 这当然是一个有前途的途径，但我们意识到一些事情：即使我们在路由器上获得了代码执行，我们也必须弄清楚它的自定义串行协议，甚至有机会与主控制盒对话。这不是黑兹尔和我想用整个假期做的事情，所以我们决定去别处看看。尝试 2：伪造电话 查看盒子内的其他终端，我们看到每个系统的 PH LINE 电话连接器。这是有希望的，因为 Frank 现有的对讲系统在工作时使用 DTMF 信号打开大门。然而，主控制盒不太可能盲目接受任何电话命令，而在用户提出要求后不主动监听它们。本来可以检验这个假设，但我们再次面临调试能力极其有限的现实，而且对电话信令系统的了解也很少。第三次是魅力：中间榛树中的人和我知道系统中一定存在一些漏洞，使我们能够将自己的命令注入门控系统。我们是对的，但我们首先需要改变观点。我们最初的假设是，我们需要对系统进行自上而下的控制，以使其按照我们的意愿运行。在我们之前未能做到这一点之后，我们改变了目标，对系统进行自下而上的控制：从根本上破坏它。我们将搜索范围从语音盒扩展到主接线盒，该接线盒在语音盒和（无法访问的）主控制器之间布线。拧下两个平头螺丝后，我们遇到了一个有趣的惊喜：一根我们没有想到的额外电缆。追踪电缆导致了一个启示：主控制箱通过接线盒控制电磁阀，这是负责解锁大门的机械装置！接触电磁阀控制线极大地改变了我们的方法。螺线管只是电磁体，有两种状态：未通电（锁定）和通电（解锁）；没有安全措施，没有可以窥探的协议。有了这个简单的接入点，我们只需将自己的电源施加到电磁阀上即可解锁门。此外，来自语音盒中终端的 12 伏直流辅助电源非常适合为微控制器供电。这是我们提出的计划：
  4. 分开连接到锁壳并触发电磁阀的电线。将分叉端连接到支持 Wi-Fi 的 ESP32 继电器板。用 Rust 编写固件，将 ESP32 变成一个 Matter 客户端，我们可以将其连接到 Frank 的 Apple Home。将电路板隐藏在小接线盒内，方便地放置在建筑物旁边，以实现最大程度的隐蔽性。将电源线插入 Doorking 语音盒并将电缆插入连接处即可为开发板供电。
  5. 添加 ESP32 后的布局图。

Original Summary

  1. My friend Frank (not his real name) hosts a lot of guests at his apartment, and his complex’s intercom is what ushers them inside. You’ve probably seen them before, they look like this: The Doorking 1834-080 intercom at Frank's apartment. Up until recently, guests could find Frank’s number in the system and give it a call. If Frank recognized the people on the line, he would press a number on his dial pad, which the controller would interpret as a signal to unlock the gate. Then, management got lazy. The complex Frank lives in failed to renew their intercom’s cellular service, so it could no longer make calls for the voice system. Even after months of asking his landlord to fix it, nothing was done. Help Arrives My other friend Hazel and I arrived to visit Frank during this outage period, and he asked us to see what we could do. Here’s what we saw: A diagram of what we saw. We inspected the top box closer, giving a promising result: it was unlocked! The general layout of the box is as follows: A diagram of the voice box with wires shown. It was impossible to ignore the massive Wi-Fi/cell router in the top corner with its admin password printed right on it (not pictured). Of course, I had to investigate. Attempt 1: The Wi-Fi Router I quickly found the network and entered the login credentials shown. Of course, they weren’t changed from the defaults. I had full admin access to the router, which was awesome, until I realized that I couldn’t do very much with its basic, locked-down interface. This almost ended my exploration, but then I realized: what about SSH? AT&T, the company that makes the routers for Doorking, is smarter than a bag of rocks in that SSH is protected on their router. Sadly for them, they lose to the bag of rocks in providing a way to download their entire system configuration from the web interface, containing a way to reset the root password to whatever you want: # This file is an exported configuration from NetComm Bovine platform based device. # Private fields are encrypted but any configuraiton entry can be manually replaced by # a plain-text variable or URI-encoded text. admin.firewall.enable;1 admin.local.enable_http;1 admin.local.enable_https;1 admin.local.ssh_enable;1 admin.local.telnetenable;1 admin.open.port; admin.password; admin.user.admin;$aM9VdmCoc5vuekVU70/Gl8iJTOujxMQo admin.user.root;$DDDgp0GJy6nB29UX7pDlrUUKDkWYqp84
  2. Wow. I now see why router vulnerabilities are so common.
  3. This was certainly a promising avenue, but we realized something: even if we gained code execution on the router, we would have to figure out its custom serial protocol to even have a chance at talking to the main control box. This wasn’t something Hazel and I wanted to spend our entire vacation doing, so we decided to look elsewhere. Attempt 2: Faking a Phone Looking at the other terminals within the box, we saw the PH LINE phone connectors for each system. This was promising, since Frank’s existing intercom system used DTMF signals to open the gate back when it was working. However, it was unlikely that the main control box would blindly accept any phone commands while not actively listening for them after a user had asked it to. It would’ve been possible to test this hypothesis, but we were again left with the reality of extremely limited debugging capabilities, in addition to minimal knowledge of phone signaling systems. Third Time’s the Charm: Man in the Middle Hazel and I knew there had to be some vulnerability in the system that would allow us to inject our own commands into the gate control system. We were correct, but we first needed a change in perspective. Our initial assumption was that we needed to take top-down control over the system to make it do what we wanted. After our previous failures to do so, we changed our goal to take bottom-up control of the system: undermining it at its core. We expanded our search past the voice box to the main junction box that routed the wires between the voice box and the (inaccessible) main controller. After unscrewing two flathead screws, we were met with an interesting surprise: an extra cable we didn’t expect. Tracing the cable led to a revelation: the main control box controls the solenoid, the mechanical device responsible for unlocking the gate, through the junction box! Having access to the solenoid control wire changed our approach dramatically. Solenoids are just electromagnets that have two states: unpowered (locked) and powered (unlocked); no security measures, no protocols to snoop. With this easy access point, we could just apply our own power to the solenoid to unlock the gate. In addition, the 12 volt DC auxiliary power from a terminal in the voice box would be perfect to power a microcontroller. Here is the plan we came up with:
  4. Split the wire that runs to the lock housing and triggers the solenoid. Connect the split end to a Wi-Fi-enabled ESP32 relay board. Write firmware in Rust to turn the ESP32 into a Matter client that we can connect to Frank’s Apple Home. Hide the board inside the little junction box, conveniently placed there by the building for maximum discreetness. Power the board by plugging a power cable into the Doorking voice box and running the cable into the junction.
  5. A diagram of the layout once we added the ESP32.

评论（默认中文）

anilakar 2026/03/24 15:22
Frank 的客人只需获取 Doorking 16120 默认钥匙即可开始开门。

编辑：十年前的本科生恶作剧：

我们大学生经营的电子实验室有一个问题：从技术上讲，任何持有学生卡的人都可以在任何特定时间进入实验室，但该部门只给了我们一小套钥匙，我们必须与其他学生协会共享。显然我们需要一个解决方案。

我们做了一些窥探，发现请求退出按钮电线与所有其他电线和管道一起在电缆桥架上运行，因为实验室位于地下室。我们选择了一个适当黑暗、不显眼的地方，并连接了一个树莓派，驱动一个晶体管，然后连接一个继电器，然后将其与该按钮并联。然后，用户可以连接到本地实验室 WiFi，然后通过 SSH 连接到设备。登录 shell 被替换为一个脚本，该脚本使 GPIO 线脉冲半秒，随后导致门打开。

我们从未被抓到，而且几年后大楼翻新时，显然所有证据都被销毁了。

Frank's guests just need to get the Doorking 16120 default key and start letting themselves in.

Edit: undergrad shenanigans from ten years ago:

Our university student-run electronics lab had an issue: technically anyone with a student card was allowed on premises at any given time, but the department only gave us a small set of keys that we had to share with the rest of the student associations. Obviously we needed a solution.

We did some snooping and found that the request-to-exit button wire was running on a cable tray alongside all the other wiring and plumbing, as the lab was in the basement. We picked a suitably dark, inconspicuous spot and wired up a Raspberry Pi driving a transistor and in turn a relay which we then wired in parallel with that button. Users could then connect to the local lab wifi and then SSH into the device. Login shell was replaced with a script that pulsed the GPIO line for half a second and subsequently caused the door to open.

We never got caught and apparently all the evidence was destroyed when the building was renovated a few years later.

在 HN 中查看
greggsy 2026/03/24 13:24
事实上，我对现在对讲机生态系统的糟糕程度感到非常惊讶。

为什么没有更多“半哑”以太网或 WiFi 产品可以让您宣布晚餐准备好了？它不需要是像这样的完全坚固耐用的商业系统，也不需要像环这样完全集成的云管理解决方案。

廉价的无名无线设备无法处理房间之间的通信，更不用说跨越房屋了。

安全隐患并非不可克服——如果网络上有多个配对代码，您可以使用配对代码。

我承认这是一个利基市场，唯一的解决方案是将 Asterix 与一些便宜的 voip 电话一起使用。

I’m actually pretty surprised how bad the intercom ecosystem is these days.

Why aren’t there more ‘semi dumb’ Ethernet or wifi products that just let you announce that dinner is ready? It doesn’t need to be a fully ruggedised commercial system like this one or a fully integrated cloud managed solution like ring.

The cheap no name wireless ones can’t handle comms between rooms, let alone across a house.

The security implications aren’t insurmountable - you could use pairing codes if there are multiple on the network.

I’ve accepted that it’s a niche market, and that the only solution is to use Asterix with a some cheapo voip phones.

在 HN 中查看
unsnap_biceps 2026/03/24 13:57
Apple 的 HomePod Mini 以及 Google Home 和 Alexa 都支持对讲模式。我想他们通常会为大多数人处理家庭案件。
Apple's HomePod Mini and Google Home and Alexa all support intercom modes. I'd presume they typically handle the home case for the majority of folks.
在 HN 中查看
pprotas 2026/03/24 15:44
HomePod Mini 是浪费钱，除非你喜欢对着永远不明白你想要它做什么的愚蠢机器人尖叫

设置定时器效果很好

HomePod Mini is a waste of money, unless you like screaming at your dumb robot that never understands what you want it to do

Setting timers works well though

在 HN 中查看
arjie 2026/03/24 14:34
我们每个房间都有 Google Home Mini，卧室和厨房都有屏幕，唯一能可靠地在房间内发送消息的就是说“嘿 Google，广播消息”，因为有一半的时间它会告诉我它还不能发送消息。如果有人知道我做错了什么，我很乐意听到它，因为这将是一个很棒的功能。

说实话，我真的厌倦了 Google Home 的做法，因为 Gemini 更新让一切都变得非常慢，而且我已经接近这样的地步：我宁愿自己部署一个可靠工作的完整系统，而不是像这样的废话。 Home Assistant 似乎有一个连接 Google Home 连接设备（例如我的百叶窗或摄像头）的功能桥，因此我应该能够保留边缘设备，但我有点想放弃整个设备并重新开始。

We have Google Home Minis in every room and the screens in bedroom and kitchen and the only thing that works reliably to message intra-room is to say "Hey Google, broadcast message" because half of the time it will tell me it can't send messages yet. If someone knows what I'm doing wrong I'd love to hear it since this would be a great feature.

To be honest, I'm honestly sick of Google Home's approach to this since the Gemini update has turned everything really slow and I'm getting close to the point where I'd rather home-roll a full system myself that works reliably instead of the crapshoot that this is. Home Assistant seems to have a functionality bridge to Google Home connected devices like my blinds or cameras so I should be able to retain the edge devices but I have half a mind to just dump the whole thing and start over.

在 HN 中查看
zer00eyz 2026/03/24 14:53
一切涉及音频的事情都是一团糟。

第一步是在房间里安装扬声器：有很多产品可以做到这一点，苹果、谷歌、Sonos。

它们中的大多数都具有一袋乐器的音质。

有大量的 D 类放大器可以连接到扬声器：Wiim、亚克力等等...这将使您花费 100 美元到 500 美元不等，而且这还是在您购买扬声器之前。其中大多数都非常适合播放音乐和投射声音。

当你使用电视时......好吧，事情会变得丑陋，因为你会想要 HDMI 弧线，并且你会想要一个中心通道，因为如果没有它，你有一半的时间可能会陷入字幕地狱。这会变得昂贵，Sonos 条形音箱要几百美元，如果您想要更好的东西……假设您可以很快让 GPU 看起来价格实惠。

现在您可以播放音频了，您如何听到它......您的手机可以正常工作，并且那里有大量的卫星。

您现在需要运行家庭助理来“中断”正在播放的内容（如果有的话）来播放您的消息，然后将其返回到当前状态。

在尝试了 WIIM、Acrylic 和一些高端立体声设备后，我只是选择了半途而废的音频质量并购买了更多 Sonos 设备。当我想真正听音乐时，我保留了一个 WIIM 设备、便宜的放大器、不错的扬声器和低音炮，但除此之外，我可以忍受 Sonos 的中等质量日常使用（而且我绝不是发烧友）。

Everything involving audio is an annoying mess.

The first step is getting speakers in a room: there are tons of products that do this, apple, google, Sonos.

Most of them have the audio quality of a bag of instruments.

There are tons of class D amps that you can hook up to speakers: Wiim, acrylic and so on... this will run you anywhere from 100 bucks to 500 and thats before you buy the speakers. Most of these will be great for playing music and projecting your voice.

The moment you involve a TV... well things get ugly because your going to want arc for HDMI and your going to want a center channel cause with out it your likely in subtitle hell half the time. This will get expensive a Sonos sound bar is a few hundred and if you want something better well... Let's say you can get to the point of making a GPU look affordable real quick.

Now that you can play audio, how do you hear it... well your phone works and there are tons of satellites out there.

You're now going to need to run home assistant to "interrupt" what ever is playing (if something is) to play your message and then return what ever it was to its current state.

After trying out WIIM, Acrylic, some high end stereo gear I just settled on half assed audio quality and bought more Sonos gear. I kept a single WIIM unit, cheap amp, decent speakers and a sub around for when I want to really listen to music but other than that I tolerate sonos' middling quality for day to day use (and I am, by no means an audiophile).

fragmede 2026/03/24 13:39
有 Butterfly 和另一家我不记得的公司，毫无疑问还有更多，它们为大型综合体提供昂贵的系统，所以利基市场是没有很多钱的小型建筑。也许pocalypse 软件可以帮助解决这个问题。
There's Butterfly and another company I can't remember and undoubtedly more, that have expensive systems for large complexes, so the niche is the small buildings that don't have a ton of money. Maybe the softwarepocalypse can help with that.
在 HN 中查看
blitzar 2026/03/24 15:37
我使用环形对讲音频​​进行类似用途。效果出奇的好，我希望有人能克隆硬件并制作一个开放版本，这样杰夫就不会每次有人按我的门铃时都听，或者在他想要的时候自己按门铃。

没有原生的苹果主页 - homebridge 可以处理这个问题。

I use a Ring Intercom Audio for a similar use. Works surprisingly well, I wish someone would clone the hardware and make an open version so Jeff didn't listen in every time someone rings my doorbell or buzz himself in whenever he wants.

No native apple home - homebridge handles that.

在 HN 中查看
sharklasers123 2026/03/24 15:37
我在阿姆斯特丹的公寓里也做了类似的事情，但技术含量较低一些。我将 Esp8266 上的继电器直接焊接到我公寓的对讲机 PCB 上的解锁按钮上。多年来一直完美工作
I did similar in my apartment in Amsterdam but a little more low-tech. I soldered the relay on an Esp8266 directly to the unlock button on the intercom PCB in my apartment. Worked flawlessly for years
在 HN 中查看
FlorinSays 2026/03/24 14:09
我也遇到了同样的问题，我已经寻找现成的解决方案一年多了，然后我找到了一个可以进行逆向工程并构建现成的板并安装在我的对讲机中的人，价格不到 30 欧元。

我不确定是否应该发布该链接，因为它是罗马尼亚特有的，但我喜欢这些 buids 的简陋： https://www.olx.ro/d/oferta/automatizare-interfon-electra-cu...

I had the same problem and I've searched for ready made solutions for over an year before I found a guy that reverse-engineers and builds ready-made boards to install in my intercom for less than 30 euro.

I'm unsure if I should post the link or not as it's specific to Romania, but I love how janky the buids are: https://www.olx.ro/d/oferta/automatizare-interfon-electra-cu...

jeffwilcox 2026/03/24 13:58
在他们将我们的产品换成 SaaS+硬件蝴蝶 mx 之前，我一直想做这样的事情。

西雅图的许多建筑物都必须更换这些门王，因为犯罪分子发现它们是多么容易被破坏。

Was always wanting to do something like this before they swapped ours out for a SaaS+hardware butterfly mx thing.

Those Doorkings have had to get replaced at so many buildings in Seattle now that criminals figured out how easy they were to override.

wzdd 2026/03/24 14:09
乐趣。我最近用我的做了这个。现在有一根不显眼的 USB 电缆从它后面延伸出来......
Fun. I did this recently with mine. There's now a discreet USB cable running down from behind it...

在 HN 中查看
sneak 2026/03/24 15:25
以真实姓名以书面形式承认重罪是疯狂的。

希望没有人决定为此打扰他们。 我不是律师，但对于这位外行来说，访问路由器并重置其 root 密码至少违反了 CFAA，并且可能存在刑事恶作剧以及窃取交流电源的行为。

你不能付钱让我写这样的文章，而且我会一直戴着手套。

Confessing to felonies, in writing, under one’s real name is wild.

Here’s hoping nobody decides to bother them about this. I’m not a lawyer but this appears to this layperson at the very least a CFAA violation by accessing the router and resetting its root password, as well as possibly criminal mischief as well as whatever stealing AC power is.

You couldn’t pay me to do a writeup like this, and I’d be wearing gloves the whole time.

在 HN 中查看
Hackbraten 2026/03/24 15:49
如果电力专门用于恢复租户付费的服务或系统，如何构成窃电？
How is it stealing power if the power is exclusively used for restoring a service or system that the tenant is paying for?
在 HN 中查看
cryptoz 2026/03/24 14:20
相关的是，我仍然对房东在对讲机中关于电话号码隐私的谎言感到不安。我在多栋公寓楼多次被告知，“不用担心，虽然当有人点击您的进入代码时系统会拨打您的电话，但您的电话号码不会被泄露”。然后你签署租约，从 Instacart 到你的新地方送货，然后发现你的“私人”号码被大声喊出，整个街区外都听到了，而且是响亮的 DTMF 音调。

BS。

Related, I'm still upset at the lies told by landlords regarding phone number privacy in buzz-in intercoms. I've been told multiple times at multiple apartment buildings, "don't worry, while the system will call your phone when someone taps your entry code, your phone number won't be revealed". And then you sign the lease, get a delivery from Instacart in your new place, and find that your 'private' number is blasted out loud, heard a whole city block away, in a loud-ass DTMF tone sequence.

BS.
